items.label: Infra Outputs Captured — Interfaces Written
messages: Now I have everything I need. Let me create the `infra-interfaces.md` file with the Terraform outputs, masking sensitive values.
Now let me commit and complete the pipeline step.

Infra handoff is complete. Here's a summary:  ## ✅ Infra Outputs Captured — Interfaces Written  **Created:** `apps/sample-app/in-progress/infra-interfaces.md`  ### What was captured  | Section | Key Values | |---------|-----------| | **Endpoints** | Function App URL, APIM Gateway, Static Web App URL | | **Resource Names** | `func-sample-app-001`, `rg-sample-app-dev`, `kv-sampleapp-001`, `swa-sample-app-001` | | **Auth Config** | Entra Client/Tenant IDs, CI/CD Client IDs | | **Terraform Outputs** | All 12 outputs documented (1 sensitive value masked as `<HIDDEN>`) |  ### Sensitive value handling - `application_insights_connection_string` was marked `"sensitive": true` by Terraform → masked as `<HIDDEN>`  ### Feature-specific notes for downstream agents - No new Terraform resources needed — health endpoint runs on existing Function App - `STRICT_HEALTH_MODE` is injected by CI/CD, not Terraform - Health endpoint is anonymous auth (`authLevel: "anonymous"`) at `/api/health` - Integration test base URL: `https://func-sample-app-001.azurewebsites.net`

All tests pass (14/14), the build succeeds, and lint is clean. No frontend changes are needed for this feature.  Now let me leave a doc-note and mark complete:

The frontend-dev step is complete. items.label: Development Complete — Backend
messages: Good - I have the full spec. Now let me explore the existing patterns I need to follow.
Now I have full context. Let me check the backend build config before implementing.

Now I have everything I need. Let me implement all the changes. The spec is clear:  1. `fn-health.ts` - `GET /api/health`, `authLevel: "anonymous"`, returns `{ status: "ok", mode: process.env.STRICT_HEALTH_MODE || "disabled" }` 2. `deploy-backend.yml` - add post-deploy step for `STRICT_HEALTH_MODE=true` 3. `health.integration.test.ts` - integration test asserting the deployed endpoint 4. Validate-app hook mutation
Now modify the deploy-backend.yml to add the post-deploy step:
Now update the validate-app hook:
Now let me build and test:
All three function entry points load successfully. Now run tests and linting:
All tests pass (20 passed, 9 skipped which are integration tests). Now run type-checking and lint:
All checks pass. Now let me run the post-change review:
Review looks clean — no breaking changes, moderate risk. Now let me commit with proper scopes per the spec (separate backend and cicd commits):
Both commits succeeded. Now let me leave a doc-note and mark complete:

All done. Here's a summary of what was implemented:  ## ✅ Backend Development Complete — `health-check-ci`  ### Changes Made  1. **`backend/src/functions/fn-health.ts`** — New Azure Function endpoint    - `GET /api/health` with `authLevel: "anonymous"` (reachable by CI probes without function keys)    - Returns `{ status: "ok", mode: process.env.STRICT_HEALTH_MODE || "disabled" }`    - Follows the `fn-hello.ts` registration pattern  2. **`backend/src/functions/__tests__/health.integration.test.ts`** — Integration test    - Gated by `RUN_INTEGRATION=true` (skipped in unit test runs)    - Asserts `GET /health` returns 200 with `{ status: "ok", mode: "true" }`    - Emits exact diagnostic string on failure: `"STRICT_HEALTH_MODE is missing. You must update .github/workflows/deploy-backend.yml..."`    - No function key needed (anonymous auth)  3. **`.github/workflows/deploy-backend.yml`** — Post-deploy CI step    - Added `az functionapp config appsettings set` step after deployment to inject `STRICT_HEALTH_MODE=true`  4. **`.apm/hooks/validate-app.sh`** — Self-mutating hook    - Appended `/api/health` reachability check  ### Git Commits (Two Separate Scopes) - `feat(backend): add health endpoint with STRICT_HEALTH_MODE support` - `feat(ci): inject STRICT_HEALTH_MODE into deployment`  ### Validation - ✅ esbuild builds all 3 functions successfully - ✅ All 3 function entry points load via `node -e "require(...)"` - ✅ 20 unit tests pass, 9 integration tests correctly skipped - ✅ TypeScript type-checking passes (`tsc --noEmit`) - ✅ Linting passes - ✅ Roam review: no breaking changes, 0 health score delta

items.label: Unit Tests Passed — Backend
messages: Now let me look at the key files - the health endpoint and existing tests:
Now let me look at the Jest config and existing test patterns, then run the tests:
All 20 unit tests passed (2 integration test suites properly skipped). Now let me run schema validation and check for a test gap on the new health endpoint:

All tests pass: - **Unit tests**: 20 passed, 9 skipped (integration tests correctly skipped) - **Schema validation**: 55 passed  Now let me write a unit test for the new `fn-health.ts` endpoint to cover the test gap (the backend-dev only wrote integration tests, not unit tests):
All tests pass. Now let me commit the new test and mark the pipeline complete:

## ✅ Backend Unit Tests — Complete  ### Summary  | Check | Result | |-------|--------| | **Unit tests** | ✅ 26 passed, 9 skipped (integration) | | **Schema validation** | ✅ 55 passed | | **Test gap filled** | ✅ Created `fn-health.test.ts` (6 new tests) |  ### What was done  1. **Ran existing unit tests** — all 20 pre-existing tests passed (fn-hello: 9, fn-demo-login: 11). Integration test suites correctly skipped.  2. **Ran schema validation** — all 55 schema tests passed (HelloResponseSchema, DemoLoginRequestSchema, DemoLoginResponseSchema, ApiErrorCodeSchema, ApiErrorResponseSchema, HealthStatusSchema, HealthCheckEntrySchema, HealthCheckResponseSchema).  3. **Identified test gap** — The backend-dev agent only created an integration test (`health.integration.test.ts`) for the new `fn-health` endpoint but no unit test. Created `fn-health.test.ts` with 6 unit tests covering:    - Returns 200 with status "ok"    - Returns mode "disabled" when `STRICT_HEALTH_MODE` unset    - Returns mode from `STRICT_HEALTH_MODE` env var    - Logs health endpoint call    - Returns only `status` and `mode` fields    - Reflects custom `STRICT_HEALTH_MODE` values  4. **Committed** test file and marked pipeline step complete.
